title: Frankenstein Identity with Graduated Credit-Building Pattern
narrative: Fraudster combines stolen SSN of a real person with fabricated name and synthetic address, creating a Frankenstein identity that passes initial credit checks. Opens thin-file account with $500-800 credit limit. Follows disciplined 90-120 day aging strategy: makes 3-5 small purchases ($15-45) monthly at grocery/gas MCCs, pays minimum on-time to build payment history. After seasoning period, applies for 2-3 additional cards within 72 hours using same synthetic profile. Once approved with $2000-5000 aggregate limits, executes rapid bust-out: 8-12 CNP transactions totaling $4200-6800 within 48 hours at electronics/jewelry merchants, all shipping to freight forwarder address (dist1 >500km from billing). TransactionAmt jumps from historical $32 average to $480-750 range. All purchases occur during business hours (txn_hour 9-17) to mimic legitimate behavior, using free email domains (gmail.com, yahoo.com) with account age <6 months.
indicators: card1_txn_count_30d increases from 4-5 to 11-14 in final 48 hours
card1_amt_zscore spikes above 8.5 (historical avg $32, final transactions $480-750)
dist1 exceeds 500km between billing addr1 and shipping address
P_emaildomain shows free provider with email account age <180 days
Velocity: 3 new credit applications within 72 hours after 90-day dormancy
ProductCD shifts from 'W' (wallet/small items) to 'C' (electronics) exclusively
All bust-out transactions are CNP with identical shipping R_addr1 matching known freight forwarder
summary: A thin-file account with 90-120 days of disciplined small purchases ($15-45 at grocery/gas) suddenly executes 8-12 high-value transactions ($480-750) within 48 hours, shipping to a distant freight forwarder address over 500km away. Transaction count jumps from 4-5 monthly to 11-14 in two days, with average purchase amount spiking from $32 to the $480-750 range.
reasoning: The fraudster deliberately builds payment history to gain credit limit increases, then immediately maxes out all available credit by purchasing high-value resellable goods shipped to a consolidation point for export. The disciplined aging period followed by coordinated bust-out across multiple newly-approved cards reveals planned financial exploitation rather than a customer experiencing sudden legitimate need.
indicator_explanations: Transaction frequency suddenly doubles in the final two days before vanishing
Purchase amounts explode to twenty times the account's normal spending level
Shipping destination is over 500 kilometers from the registered billing address
Email comes from free service and account was created less than six months ago
Three new credit lines requested within three days after months of silence
Purchases shift abruptly from low-value items to high-value electronics only
All card-not-present orders ship to same address flagged as freight forwarding hub
